title: cochlidiosperma buxbaumii
intro: Cochlidiosperma buxbaumii, also known as the Buxbaum's false penny-cress or Buxbaum's false cress, is a rare annual plant species in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States, particularly in Arizona, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in the treatment of various health issues, such as respiratory problems and skin conditions. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea or tincture, and may be used to treat various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
